name: Jaime Alexander Aardfom
appearance: He has a sharp, youthful face with golden-blonde curls, pointed ears, and a vibrant, wide-eyed expression of joyous excitement with the overall look of a high-born adventurer who finds genuine, perhaps even reckless, delight in the chaotic nature of his magic

traits: The noble blood in my veins entitles me to some things. I don't believe in working hard.
Some people are just better than others and the most valuable skill is the ability to rule people.
Most laws are for the common people, I am above most regulations.
ideals: I want to be best friends with my patron. I just need to earn her respect first
While I'm in this rough situation, I want to gather trusted servants who I can trust when I get back to power.
Though my dad won't allow it, I want to be the head of the family after he passes as the oldest son.
flaws: I'm too scared to confront my dad and even after 1.5 years away, I would still obey his orders in a heartbeat.
Even though I think myself to be better than commoners, I'm too afraid to confront them about it.
I panic when I'm close to a woman, I'm scared that people will find out that I never even kissed one.

organizations: Bannermen of the Aardfom family across all the elven lands: Me being denied of my inheritance would bring shame and shake our family's seat. Even though my father's opinions seem to be final, it is still a family secret. I could still have the hospitality of many great houses if I wanted to. I would have to not ask for much or for simple day-to-day things to avoid suspicion. (Not gonna break the game dw DM)
allies: My siblings: Fiona (20), the oldest of the 6 of us: Fiona left the care of our family at her own will. She is part of a big pirate crew and holds great responsibility. It is impossible to send letters to one another when she is escaping from the law, but we try to meet once every 4 months. I'm the only trusted relative of hers because I didn't snitch on her when I was 7 and she was 9 about her pet wolf. She missed our last meeting but I sometimes hear about her well-being in big docks. Mara (20), drew the short straw: She inherited my father's skin disease at birth, causing her to constantly care about her exposure to the sun. She often stays indoors in her room, painting and reading. She loves to dance whenever an event is being held at our home. Orion (13), the oldest male after me: Orion pretends to love and respect me, but I hate him. He is my father's lap dog and probably the reason why I got shooed away. It is pretty funny for Dad to consider him a mastermind when he never beat me in a game of chess. Elara (12), will grow up gorgeous: I feel like Elara is the only one of us siblings who didn't encounter father's wrath and disappointment. She was a natural from the start. Her needlework, manners, bright teeth and beautiful eyes all convinced my parents to raise her to be a bride. Even before her body started to mature Mother was in contact with the royal family to arrange a marriage. Adrian (7), unshaped clay: it is too early to talk about Adrian's personality. He has the same color of eyes and hair as me. I'm just hoping that Mother doesn't make the same mistakes she did with the rest of us.

notes: Jamie is unable to grow facial hair, making it necessary for him to shave regularly to avoid looking awkward.
backstory: It was a cause of joy and celebration when I was born because the Aardfom family and my father's blood were finally guaranteed to continue after 2 daughters and 1 son who died at birth. However, the feeling of pride quickly vanished as I grew up. It probably didn't help that my father was forced to marry a human to be an example of recent times. I was never good at holding a sword even though I had the best practitioners available. I didn't have any natural skill for magic, nor the capacity to quickly grasp it. I didn't have a strong connection to nature, shadows or faith. I was simply mundane except for my surname. Before giving up on me for good, my father also tried to train me as his second. I joined many ballads, dinners and meetings. Courted with not only noblewomen but also with a dozen princesses. I was better at this than I was with the sword or with the magic, but I wasn't up to my father's standards. When he left me to find out what I was good at when I was 14, I spent the following few years building an echo chamber of lesser nobles who would back all my needless desires and justify all my pity excuses. When I turned 16 it was about time that I became a serious figure in the family and even the king of Kheranthe considered me a candidate for her youngest daughter, I ruined all the respect given to me by my name with the self-entitlement I built. My father was very disappointed and decided to get rid of me. A part of me still believes at first he wanted me to find out who I am but I probably got that idea out of his head when I ran out of my purse and asked for more 2 weeks after my departure. Though I was unsuccessful in satisfying the King or my father at those times, I connected with Princess Lyraesia well. She also crumbled under the pressure of her perfectionist father, she also didn't have any skills to prove herself and that left her with no options other than having a political marriage her father decided on.

One of my regrets is that I lost an auction for a painting. My father didn't let me go any higher but I liked it a lot. I'm still interested in buying it, although my two attempts since then were ridiculed by the owner. They think I only pretend to understand the painting and consider my desire to be about ego. They made sure to add a few comments about me being a half-elf and a very young kid. I'm used to hearing this kind of criticism a lot since I grew up in Kheranthe so I don't mind it that much. But I still desire that painting. Weirdly I can still see it whenever I focus my thoughts on it. It's like we have a connection. It even visited me in a dream, where the horse in the painting spoke to me in riddles.

Fiona, unlike me, never felt like he belonged with the nobles and riches. She was always a free spirit and when our father tried to kill her pet wolf because it wasn't 'suitable for a lady nor a castle' she just got out. Mother never supported her desires, she also didn't stop our father from hitting her constantly. When I got kicked out the first thing I did was to try and reach her. We met soon after at Feion as she is a highly wanted fugitive in Kheranthe because of her profession as a pirate and our father's desire for control. That's when I met her girlfriend Arianna. Arianna is one of the highest-ranked people within the fleet. She is a water Genasi and is devoted to Yrchenn. Even though I'm worried about my dear sister at times, I feel better knowing that Arianna is by her side. She was the one who came up with the solution that Fiona and I should meet once every 4 months, At Buibus 4th and Fexyius 4th at Feion. Unfortunately, I never saw her again, she is too valuable for the entire operation to afford more than a day or two on solid ground. Last time I met Fiona, she mentioned that Arianna had mentioned the idea of marriage a few times and I gave her my full support.

My first adventure was a dungeon, I now realize it was well beyond my capabilities and I'm lucky that I survived. Since I left home with a large sum of money in my hand, I recruited 5 mercenaries to accompany me. 4 of them died and the last one is now retired because he is missing a leg. I had to drag him out, admittedly I wetted my pants during the fight. Originally I thought I would figure it out as I went but that encounter assured me that I needed a shortcut. A day after dragging the surviving mercenary out I went back in and sought the attention of something bigger and better than me by the bones that gathered within that dungeon. I formed a pact upon my soul with a being beyond my understanding and admittedly I don't remember all the rules. My patron put a thing in our agreement that started 'with when you get back your noble seat' but I can't remember what it was about, I remember feeling a certain joy when hearing it with such certainty. All I know is that sometimes she tells me to choose a certain path when given options and I don't have the luxury to deny it often.

My next adventure was even more extreme. It was a few minutes after my pact and was suggested to me by my Patron. She put a location in my hand, an itching feeling that I couldn't scratch as long as I didn't arrive there. I traveled for 2 days and nights and when I arrived at the tomb at that location she told me to go in. With some sort of magic, she got rid of my need for sleep and I fought some sort of monster and obtained a necklace. Those two days and that fight were the only times I got constant orders, and I feel like I was mainly given this power to succeed in that certain mission. Orders have become very rare after that, and I couldn't even reach out to her whenever I wanted to. However, the tactics and knowledge that she shared with me for those 2 days were an incredible experience. It motivated me to gain her respect and I now realize what a long path that will be. At least now she almost certainly answers when I seek her attention and has given me a bit more power.

Once, I was pulled to a very hot plane by my Patron to cheer for a certain champion in a free-for-all arena. I was filled with determination to be the fanatic of this champion I saw for the first time so much that I didn't look at any of the other spectators. I screamed so much and moved too frequently that I had to sleep through the next two weeks. The joy I felt when tens of thousands of participants fought, only for the one I cheered for to win was magnificent.

For a short while, I was used to using my name to stay at a mansion and have some wonderful food. It is an easy process, I just say I'm doing something around 'this' part of Kheranthe at my father's command and decided to visit them to see if they needed anything. This has gotten way harder with time since I haven't been in anything connected to my family in a year. I stopped doing it after one time I accidentally came across Princess Lyraesia. She was sent there for a real reason and I felt so embarrassed with my adventurer clothes next to her royal dress. I'm used to not having my needs satisfied in an aristocratic manner, but the drawback is that everyone can tell. I didn't smell extraordinary, my nails weren't polished and all that stuff just felt shameful since I genuinely wanted to have her respect. Neither of us luxury to not consider political outcomes when it comes to love life. In that context, she is the only person I met that I consider to be fitting for me.

Then, my adventures got more grounded. I even gathered enough money to send back to my father as an apology. I didn't get a response nor expected one. I decided to follow Fiona's advice and be one with the common folk. I stopped being in a rush to return home as it was my destiny and collected some of the best memories of my life. I tricked a forest fairy near Fex (I both answered her riddle and asked her a better one), I outfoxed a druid who spent most of her time wild-shaped into a fox (I believe he still holds a grudge), I gambled and won in a game that had a success rate of 1 in 100.000 (This was against my patron's orders) and helped a changeling find his real face (I wish I didn't, he immediately combusted and turned to ash as he wore his true face).
